sections.heading: Subject-Verb Agreement
sections.explanation: The subject and verb in a sentence MUST match. Singular subject → singular verb. Plural subject → plural verb.

basic_rule.description: Singular subjects use is/was/has/does/verb+s. Plural subjects use are/were/have/do/verb.

tricky_situations.title: Words between subject and verb
tricky_situations.note: Don't be fooled by words in the middle!

examples.wrong: The box of chocolates are delicious.
examples.right: The box of chocolates is delicious.

examples.wrong: The students in the class was quiet.
examples.right: The students in the class were quiet.

examples.wrong: A team of doctors have arrived.
examples.right: A team of doctors has arrived.

tricky_situations.memory_trick: Cover the middle words. 'The box [] is delicious.' → Correct!

tricky_situations.title: Collective Nouns (group words)
tricky_situations.note: In American English → usually singular verb
examples: The team is ready to play.
The family has moved to a new house.
The committee has made a decision.

tricky_situations.title: Compound Subjects with AND
examples: The cat and the dog were playing.
Rice and curry are my favorite food.

exception.note: When two things = one unit → singular
examples: Bread and butter is my breakfast. (one combined food item)
Law and order is important. (one concept)

sections.heading: Tense Consistency
sections.explanation: Don't switch tenses in the middle of writing without a good reason!

wrong_example.text: Yesterday, I went to the market. I see a beautiful dress and decide to buy it. I was happy about my purchase.

sections.correct_example: Yesterday, I went to the market. I saw a beautiful dress and decided to buy it. I was happy about my purchase.

tenses.name: Past Tense
tenses.regular_rule: Add -ed: walk → walked, talk → talked, play → played
tenses.example: Yesterday, I went to school and ate lunch with my friend.

tenses.name: Present Tense
examples: She walks to school every day.
I usually wake up at 7 a.m.
I will visit my grandmother tomorrow.
She will start her new job next Monday.
They will finish the project soon.
I am going to visit my grandmother.
She is going to start her new job.

sections.memory_trick: Past = it's DONE (the 'd' in 'done' like '-ed'). Present = PRESENT moment (it's here NOW). Future = WILL happen (will = future helper)

sections.heading: Their / There / They're
sections.explanation: These three words sound IDENTICAL but mean completely different things!

words.use_when: Something BELONGS to a group of people
examples: The team won their first match.
My parents sold their old car.
The children ate their lunch quickly.
words.memory_trick: Replace 'their' with 'our'. If it makes sense → use THEIR. 'Students forgot our homework' → makes sense!

words.meaning: A place OR starting a sentence
examples_location: I have never been there before.
examples_starting: There is a problem with the plan.
There are many reasons to be happy.
There was a big storm last night.
There were 500 people at the concert.
words.memory_trick: 'THERE' contains 'HERE'. Both relate to PLACE (here and there). 'Put the book over HERE/THERE'

examples: They're going to the party tonight. (They are going to the party tonight.)
They're my best friends. (They are my best friends.)
I think they're right about this.
words.memory_trick: Replace with 'they are'. If it makes sense → use THEY'RE.

combined_practice: They're going to their house over there.
There are their books on the table.
They're happy with their new home there.

sections.heading: Your / You're & To / Too / Two

examples: You're doing a great job! (You are doing a great job!)
words.memory_trick: Replace with 'you are'. 'You are bag?' → doesn't make sense → YOUR. 'You are doing great' → makes sense → YOU'RE

examples_also: She is tired, and I am too.

sections.combined_practice: The two of us walked to the park, and it was too far to walk back, too.

sentences.text: The group of students are going on a trip tomorrow and they was very excited.
sentences.answer: The group of students is going on a trip tomorrow and they were very excited.

sentences.text: Yesterday I go to the market, buy vegetables, and cook a meal. It taste really good.
sentences.answer: Yesterday I went to the market, bought vegetables, and cooked a meal. It tasted really good.

sentences.text: There going to there house over their.
sentences.answer: They're going to their house over there.

sentences.text: You're friend called and said your to busy to come.
sentences.answer: Your friend called and said you're too busy to come.
